Ref: {{ref}}
Post-Close Tax Reminder
ESOP Plan Tax Filing & §1042 Election Coordination
Company
{{companyName}}
Date
{{sentDate}}
CPA
{{cpaName}}
Tax Year
{{taxYear}}
Purpose: This reminder outlines the key tax filing requirements and deadlines for the {{companyName}} ESOP following the transaction close on {{closingDate}}. Please review each item and confirm your coordination timeline with Forhemit.
§ 01ESOP Plan Tax Filing Requirements

As the ESOP trust is now a qualified retirement plan under IRC §401(a), the following annual filings are required:

A.Form 5500 — Annual Return/Report. Due the last day of the seventh month after the plan year ends (July 31 for calendar-year plans). First filing covers the short plan year from {{closingDate}} to December 31, {{taxYear}}. Must include Schedule H (financial information) and Schedule I (small plan financial information, if applicable).
B.Form 8955-SSA — Annual Registration Statement. Due the last day of the seventh month after the plan year ends. Reports participants who separated from service with a deferred vested benefit. Coordinate with the plan recordkeeper for participant data.
C.Form 5500-SF (if eligible). Plans with fewer than 100 participants and meeting other criteria may file the simplified Form 5500-SF. Confirm eligibility with the plan's third-party administrator.
D.Trust tax return — Form 1120 or 990-T. The ESOP trust itself may need to file if it has unrelated business taxable income (UBTI). Consult with the trust's tax advisor on filing requirements.
E.State filings. Verify any state-level retirement plan registration or reporting requirements applicable to {{companyState}}. Some states require additional disclosures for employee benefit plans.
§ 02§1042 Election Status

If the selling shareholder elected to defer capital gains under IRC §1042, the following deadlines and requirements apply:

{{sellerName}}
{{closingDate}}
{{saleProceeds}}
{{section1042Deadline}}
{{qrpStatus}}
A.Election timing. The §1042 election must be made on the seller's original tax return for the year of the sale (or on a timely filed amended return). For {{taxYear}}, the deadline is {{section1042Deadline}}.
B.Qualified replacement property (QRP). The seller must acquire QRP within a 3-month window before or after the sale date. QRP includes domestic operating company stocks and bonds. Verify that the seller's investment qualifies and that the holding period requirements are documented.
C.C corporation requirement. The company must be a C corporation at the time of the sale. If the company converted from an LLC, confirm the conversion was effective before the ESOP transaction date.
D.30% ownership threshold. The ESOP must acquire at least 30% of the company's outstanding stock. Confirm this threshold was met with the independent appraiser.
E.C recapture exposure. If any of the seller's stock was acquired before 1984 and has C corporation earnings and profits, the §1042 election may not be available for those shares. Review the seller's basis schedule with your tax counsel.
§ 03Company Contribution Timing

Contributions to the ESOP trust are tax-deductible to the company under IRC §404, subject to the following limits and timing rules:

{{annualContributionLimit}}
{{currentYearContributions}}
{{contributionType}}
A.Deductible limit. The company may deduct contributions up to 25% of covered payroll for stock contributions, or 25% for cash contributions used to repay ESOP loan principal. Combined limit for stock and cash: 25% of covered payroll.
B.Cash contributions for loan repayment. Cash used to repay ESOP acquisition debt is deductible up to 10% of covered payroll in addition to the 25% stock contribution limit. Verify the loan amortization schedule and timing of payments.
C.Timing for tax year {{taxYear}}. Contributions must be made by the due date of the company's tax return (including extensions) to be deductible for {{taxYear}}. For calendar-year filers: {{companyFilingDeadline}}.
D.Stock vs. cash contributions. If the company contributed stock to the ESOP (e.g., newly issued shares), the deduction equals the fair market value at the time of contribution. For cash contributions, the deduction is the cash amount. Document the contribution date, type, and FMV for the trust records.
E.Excess contributions. If contributions exceed the 25% limit, the excess may be carried forward and deducted in subsequent years. Track any carry-forward amounts and report on the company's tax return.
§ 04Participant Statements

Under ERISA, the plan administrator must provide annual benefit statements to ESOP participants. Coordinate with the plan recordkeeper to ensure timely distribution:

{{totalParticipants}}
{{statementDeadline}}
{{recordkeeperName}}
A.Quarterly statements. Participants must receive quarterly statements within 45 days of the end of each quarter, showing account balance and allocation of contributions.
B.Annual statement. The annual statement must include the participant's total account balance, vested balance, diversification rights (for participants age 55+ with 10+ years of service), and a summary of plan terms.
C.Diversification notice. Participants who are age 55 or older and have completed 10 years of plan participation must be notified annually of their right to diversify up to 25% (50% in the sixth year) of their account balance.
D.Summary plan description (SPD). If the ESOP is new, the SPD must be distributed to all participants within 90 days of becoming a participant. Confirm the SPD has been finalized and distributed.
§ 05Key Deadlines — {{taxYear}}

The following table summarizes the critical tax filing and compliance deadlines for the {{companyName}} ESOP:

Deadline Action Required Responsible Party
{{qrpDeadline}}QRP acquisition window closes (§1042)Seller + CPA
{{firstForm5500Due}}First Form 5500 due (short plan year)TPA + CPA
{{participantStatementQ1}}Q1 participant statements dueRecordkeeper
{{participantStatementQ2}}Q2 participant statements dueRecordkeeper
{{diversificationNotice}}Diversification notice to eligible participantsPlan administrator
{{companyFilingDeadline}}Company tax return due (extensions apply)CPA
{{section1042Deadline}}§1042 election filing deadlineCPA + Seller
{{annualAppraisalDue}}Annual FMV appraisal dueTrustee + Appraiser
Extension note: If the company files for an automatic extension (Form 7004), the contribution and filing deadlines are extended accordingly. Confirm extension filing with your CPA to preserve deduction timing flexibility.
§ 06Contact

For questions about ESOP tax filing requirements or coordination with Forhemit's stewardship team, please contact:
